title: jovibarba globifera subsp. arenaria
intro: Jovibarba globifera subsp. arenaria, also known as the 'Snowdon Bells' or 'Snowdon Bellflower', is a rare, alpine plant species that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Jovibarba globifera subsp. arenaria include reducing inflammation, improving lung function, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions of the leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or used in salves for topical application.
